prompt: Write a warm greeting to a new colleague in five different languages (your choice). After each, add a one-line note about what's culturally implied by the register you chose.
response:
English:
Welcome aboard! I'm excited to work together—please reach out if I can help you get settled.
Note: Friendly, first-name-basis tone common in many English-speaking workplaces; approachable without being too informal.

Spanish:
¡Qué gusto tenerte en el equipo! Me alegra que te unas; cuenta conmigo para lo que necesites mientras te integras.
Note: Uses tú for a warm, modern collegial vibe; usted would be the safer formal choice with seniors or in conservative settings.

French:
Bienvenue parmi nous ! Je suis ravi(e) de travailler avec vous ; n’hésitez pas à me solliciter pour toute question.
Note: Polite vous keeps professional distance while staying warm; tutoiement typically follows mutual agreement.

Japanese:
チームへようこそ！これから一緒に働けるのを楽しみにしています。何かあれば、いつでも声をかけてくださいね。
Note: Desu/masu-level politeness signals respect and friendliness; plain form would feel too casual at first.

Arabic (MSA):
أهلاً وسهلاً بك في الفريق! يسعدني أننا سنعمل معاً، وإذا احتجت أي مساعدة فأنا هنا لدعمك.
Note: Formal-neutral MSA is courteous and widely understood; many shift to local dialects for warmer, informal chat later.
